Sui Overflow 2026 Walrus Track

AnamPouch

A patient-owned, privacy-first health memory layer — encrypted in the browser, stored on decentralised Walrus, and access-controlled on-chain with Sui Move.

zkLogin — frictionless sign-in
Walrus — encrypted blob storage
Sui Move — on-chain access control
Seal — threshold key release
Problem → Solution → Why Us

Your health data should belong to you

The Problem

Fragmented records. Notes and prescriptions are scattered across clinics; patients can't carry a complete history between providers.

AI vs privacy. Using AI on medical documents normally means uploading raw PHI to a central cloud — a leak and compliance risk.

Sharing is irreversible. A PDF or cloud link, once shared, can't be expired, time-locked, or revoked.

Our Solution

Encrypt before it leaves. Records are encrypted in the browser (AES-GCM-256); plaintext never touches the cloud.

Store & anchor. The encrypted blob lives on Walrus; a Sui Move anchor records its Blob ID and hash, tamper-evident.

Grant on-chain. Doctors get time-boxed, revocable access enforced by Seal — not by an honour system.

Why Us

The patient holds the key — not a platform, not a hospital, not us.

Cloud AI without exposing PHI — sensitive fields are redacted before any model sees them.

Zero friction both sides — patients sign in with Google (zkLogin); doctors pay no gas and need no wallet (Enoki).

Live Demo · How It Works

Two flows, one self-custodial vault

Patient — create a record

1 · LOGINzkLogin via Google → non-custodial Sui wallet
↓
2 · AIOCR + summary; PHI redacted before the cloud LLM
↓
3 · ENCRYPTAES-GCM-256 in-browser — plaintext never leaves the device
↓
4 · ANCHORBlob → Walrus; create_anchor PTB stores Blob ID + hash on Sui

Doctor — consume a grant

1 · SCANOpen the access-grant QR — no wallet, no gas (Enoki sponsored)
↓
2 · VERIFYSui Move + Clock check TTL and revocation state
↓
3 · RELEASESeal releases the key only if policy passes → fetch & decrypt
↓
4 · REVOKEPatient revokes on-chain → the key server refuses, instantly
Future Vision

From a vault to a health memory agent

On-device health agent. Reads your decrypted Walrus history locally to answer questions on drug interactions, allergies and recent trends — privacy intact.

Delegated mandates on-chain. An AgentMandate Move module lets patients grant an AI assistant scoped, time-limited read access.

Permanent, auditable trail. Every analysis, reconciliation and doctor view is logged on Sui and stored encrypted on Walrus — tamper-proof compliance records.

Cross-institution care. The same grant model scales from one doctor to clinics, labs and insurers — without ever centralising the data.

🦭
"Walrus gives AI persistent memory. AnamPouch makes sure that health memory belongs to the patient."
